plan.action: Audit lib/rate_limit.sh: verify rate_limit_detect regex, rate_limit_parse_wait patterns, rate_limit_backoff formula, and claude_invoke retry loop match all acceptance criteria. Confirm claude is invoked with -p --dangerously-skip-permissions.
Audit all agent caller libs to confirm they pipe prompts through claude_invoke instead of calling claude directly. Fix any direct claude calls.
Source lib/rate_limit.sh in clippy.sh if not already present so CLIPPY_RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_BASE and CLIPPY_RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES are exported at startup.

Run the full test suite (bats tests/) and fix any failing tests in tests/rate_limit.bats. Ensure the make_rate_limit_stub never emits 'rate limit' text without exit code != 0 to avoid detection loops.
Verify tests/loop.bats and tests/planning.bats still pass after rate_limit integration — no test regressions.

testing_recommendations: All tests in tests/rate_limit.bats must pass: rate_limit_detect (positive/negative/case-insensitive), rate_limit_parse_wait (seconds/minutes/fallback), rate_limit_backoff (base * attempt), claude_invoke (success, non-rate-limit failure no-retry, rate-limit retry then success, max-retries exceeded, stderr logging).
Set CLIPPY_RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_BASE=0 in every test setup to prevent real sleeps.
Use counter file pattern for multi-call stubs — never pipe to run; use run function <<< input pattern.
Stub claude must NOT emit 'rate limit' text for non-rate-limit failure paths to avoid triggering detection loop.
Smoke-test claude_invoke integration: at least one test in tests/planning.bats or tests/architect.bats should confirm claude is called via claude_invoke.
Typecheck: run shellcheck on lib/rate_limit.sh and all modified libs.
risks: The make_rate_limit_stub in tests/rate_limit.bats uses exit code 0 with rate-limit text — claude_invoke now only retries on non-zero exit + rate-limit content, so stubs must exit non-zero to trigger retry path.
Direct claude calls in agent libs that bypass claude_invoke will silently skip rate-limit handling — must audit all six libs.
bash 3.2 (macOS default) compatibility: no associative arrays, no mapfile; existing patterns already work around this.
